title: casuarina campestris subsp. tessellata
intro: Casuarina campestris subsp. tessellata, also known as the 'Tessellated Sheoak', is a species of flowering plant in the family Casuarinaceae. This plant is native to Western Australia and is traditionally used by the Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Casuarina campestris subsp. tessellata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe wounds, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical applications, such as creams or ointments, to utilize its medicinal properties.
